ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for seafood pizza; SNOMED CT coverage of composite prepared dishes is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of seafood pizza is approximately 2 slices (about 200 g), providing around 482 kcal, 25.8 g protein, and 916 mg sodium.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, seafood — Health Promotion Board Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry for Pizza (seafood), cr_id F05020080. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for seafood pizza in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 60 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: pizza with cheese and tomato topping (GI ~60, medium category) as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI table. The seafood topping does not materially alter the GI relative to the wheat-dough and tomato-sauce base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (60) × carbohydrate per serving (28.48 g/100g × 200 g serving = 56.96 g) / 100 = 34.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 459 mg/100g — exceeds 400 mg/100g threshold; potassium 180 mg/100g and fibre 0.9 g/100g provide insufficient offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit seafood pizza to no more than 1 slice per occasion and request reduced-cheese, no-added-salt preparation to keep sodium intake below 600 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 28.48 g/100g with low dietary fibre (0.9 g/100g) from refined wheat base yields a medium-to-high glycaemic load; estimated GI 60
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict seafood pizza to 1 thin-crust slice, pair with a non-starchy vegetable side, and monitor postprandial glucose, as the refined wheat base drives a significant glycaemic response despite the protein contribution from seafood.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 459 mg/100g and saturated fat ~3.2 g/100g from cheese elevate cardiovascular risk; trans fat 0.1 g/100g is present
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, limit seafood pizza to occasional consumption (1 slice), choose thin-crust wholemeal-base options, and request minimal cheese to reduce saturated fat and sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade tomato sauce and trace trans fat (0.1 g/100g) are pro-inflammatory; refined wheat base contributes to inflammatory load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are nightshade-sensitive should avoid seafood pizza; if consumed, maximise oily-fish toppings and request a wholemeal base to partially improve the anti-inflammatory profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Seafood pizza is a baked flatbread dish consisting of a refined wheat dough base spread with tomato sauce, topped with melted cheese and a mixture of seafood such as shrimp, squid, and white fish, then oven-baked until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling. The texture combines a crisp-to-chewy crust with a moist, savoury topping. The flavour profile is umami-rich from the seafood and cheese, with mild acidity from the tomato sauce. Per 100 g, it provides 241 kcal, 12.9 g protein, 8.35 g fat, and 28.48 g carbohydrate, with 459 mg sodium — a notably high sodium load driven by cheese, tomato sauce, and the inherent sodium content of seafood. Dietary fibre is low at 0.9 g/100g, reflecting the refined wheat base. The estimated glycaemic index is 60 (medium), yielding a high glycaemic load per standard 200 g serving (~34). Clinically, seafood pizza is unsuitable for hypertension (high sodium), type 2 diabetes (high glycaemic load, low fibre), and should be consumed cautiously by those managing cardiovascular disease (sodium and saturated fat). The seafood component contributes beneficial protein and some omega-3 fatty acids, partially offsetting the inflammatory and glycaemic concerns of the refined base. In Singapore and globally, seafood pizza is a popular casual dining and takeaway option, commonly served as a main meal or shared dish.
common_user_queries: How many calories in seafood pizza?
Is seafood pizza high in sodium?
What is the glycaemic index of pizza?
Is seafood pizza suitable for hypertension?
How much protein is in seafood pizza?

confidence_reason: Core macros and secondary nutrients sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id F05020080) with confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (pizza with cheese and tomato, GI ~60) as no exact seafood pizza GI entry exists; this is the closest defensible approved-source analogue. Saturated fat estimated at 3.2 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for comparable cheese-topped pizza (NDB 21299); potassium 180 mg/100g and trans fat 0.1 g/100g carried forward from input health_suitability reasoning and cross-checked against USDA data for similar pizza products.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for seafood pizza not reported in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database; USDA FoodData Central does not provide a sufficiently close exact match for this specific product. calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not available in the HPB source record for this entry. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on input health_suitability reasoning and cross-checked against USDA data for comparable cheese-topped pizza products. potassium_mg 180 mg/100g carried forward from input health_suitability reasoning and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central for comparable pizza. saturated_fat_g 3.2 g/100g estimated from USDA FoodData Central (Pizza with cheese, NDB 21299) as the closest approved-source analogue; HPB record does not report saturated fat separately. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (pizza with cheese and tomato topping); no direct seafood pizza GI entry exists in any approved GI source. Halal status not confirmed — seafood toppings may be halal but cheese and sauce sourcing is unverified; is_halal set to false as a conservative default. This is a Western dish (Italian origin, globally adapted); multilingual naming rules for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes do not apply — local_names fields are null.
